summary: The review of "CDA example 3.pdf" reveals a moderate to high-risk posture, with several clauses flagged as amber and red due to non-compliance with UoA's Preferred or Acceptable Contracting Positions. Key issues include insufficient definitions of confidential information, lack of clarity on disclosure rights to agents and affiliates, and the absence of specified durations for confidentiality obligations. Additionally, problematic language around document destruction, governing law, and dispute resolution requires immediate attention. It is recommended to escalate this agreement to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts for a detailed review and redrafting to ensure alignment with UoA standards.

flags.clause_title: 1 Introduction. The Parties have entered or propose to enter discussions
flags.snippet: 1 Introduction. The Parties have entered or propose to enter discussions option destroys all documents containing Confidential Information and any other in order to explore mutually beneficial business opportunities (th…
flags.rationale: The clause touches on Confidentiality but includes problematic language regarding the destruction of documents containing Confidential Information. This deviates materially from UoA Position #POS-11, which requires the definition of Confidential Information to exclude public, independently developed, or previously known information, and allows UoA to disclose to agents and Affiliates. Additionally, the clause does not specify a duration for confidentiality obligations, which is required to align with the Preferred or Acceptable tiers. Escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts is necessary to address these issues.

flags.clause_title: 3 Use of Confidential Information.
flags.snippet: 3 Use of Confidential Information. partnership of any kind between the Parties.
flags.rationale: The clause text is incomplete and does not provide sufficient detail to assess alignment with UoA Position #POS-11 on Confidentiality. Specifically, it does not address the required exclusions for confidential information (e.g., public information, independently developed information, etc.), the ability for UoA to disclose to agents and Affiliates, or the duration of confidentiality obligations. This lack of clarity materially deviates from the Preferred and Acceptable tiers, requiring escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts for review.

flags.clause_title: 3.1 The Recipient shall keep Confidential Information strictly
flags.snippet: 3.1 The Recipient shall keep Confidential Information strictly
flags.rationale: The clause is incomplete and does not provide sufficient detail to determine compliance with UoA Position #POS-11 on Confidentiality. Specifically, it lacks key elements such as the definition of Confidential Information (e.g., exclusions for public, independently developed, or previously known information), the ability for UoA to disclose to agents and Affiliates, and a time limitation on confidentiality obligations (e.g., up to 5 years post-completion). This falls outside the Acceptable tier and requires escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts for review and amendment.

flags.clause_title: 3.3 The Recipient may disclose the Confidential Information only to
flags.snippet: 3.3 The Recipient may disclose the Confidential Information only to the Recipient or any third party arising out of the correctness or
flags.rationale: The clause is incomplete and ambiguous, making it impossible to determine whether it aligns with UoA's Preferred or Acceptable positions on confidentiality. Specifically, it does not clarify the scope of disclosure, whether UoA can disclose to agents and Affiliates, or whether the definition of confidential information excludes public, independently developed, or previously known information. Additionally, the clause does not specify a time limit for confidentiality obligations. This falls outside the Acceptable tier and requires escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts for review and clarification.

flags.clause_title: “Purpose”). In connection with the Purpose, either Party may disclose
flags.snippet: “Purpose”). In connection with the Purpose, either Party may disclose or relating to Confidential Information. Upon the request of the disclosing Party, an information of a confidential nature to the other Party which is then officer of the Recipient shall promptly certify in writing that such destruction has been completed.
flags.rationale: The clause touches on the topic of Confidentiality (UoA Position #POS-11) but does not align with either the Preferred or Acceptable tiers. It lacks key elements such as the exclusion of information already public, independently developed, previously known by the receiving party, or legally required to be disclosed. Additionally, there is no mention of UoA's ability to disclose to agents and Affiliates, nor is the duration of confidentiality obligations specified. These omissions materially deviate from UoA's Preferred and Acceptable positions, requiring escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts.

flags.clause_title: 4 Right to Notice. In the event the Recipient receives a request to release
flags.snippet: 4 Right to Notice. In the event the Recipient receives a request to release validity or termination, shall be determined by arbitration in London in
flags.rationale: The clause touches on Dispute Resolution but does not align with UoA's Preferred or Acceptable positions. Arbitration in London is inconsistent with the Preferred position, which requires escalation to senior management, mediation, and proceedings in English, with no prevention of urgent interlocutory action through courts. The clause also does not meet the Acceptable position, as it does not indicate that it is part of a non-negotiable NZ or International Funding Agency contract. Escalation to the Senior Manager Research Grants and Contracts or General Counsel is required.

flags.clause_title: 7 Term. Either Party may terminate this Agreement on thirty (30) days’
flags.snippet: 7 Term. Either Party may terminate this Agreement on thirty (30) days’ confidential and not disclose such Confidential Information to
flags.rationale: The clause appears to conflate termination rights with confidentiality obligations, which are distinct topics under UoA Positions. Termination provisions must align with UoA Position #POS-19, which requires mutual two-way termination for material breach or insolvency, and clear rights for UoA to terminate for non-payment. Confidentiality provisions must align with UoA Position #POS-11, which specifies exclusions for public, independently developed, or legally required disclosures, and limits obligations to 5–7 years post-termination. This clause does not clearly address either topic in compliance with UoA Positions and requires escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts for review and redrafting.

flags.clause_title: 8 Disclaimer. The disclosing Party discloses the Confidential Information
flags.snippet: 8 Disclaimer. The disclosing Party discloses the Confidential Information
flags.rationale: The clause text is incomplete and does not provide sufficient detail to assess alignment with UoA's Preferred or Acceptable positions on Confidentiality (POS-11). Specifically, it lacks any definition of confidential information, exclusions for public or independently developed information, or a time limit for confidentiality obligations. This creates a material deviation from UoA's Preferred and Acceptable positions, requiring escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts for review and potential revision.

flags.clause_title: written notice. The Parties’ confidentiality obligations will survive for an
flags.snippet: written notice. The Parties’ confidentiality obligations will survive for an
flags.rationale: The clause is incomplete and does not specify the duration of confidentiality obligations, which is a critical requirement under UoA Position #POS-11. The Preferred Position requires confidentiality obligations to be limited in time (up to 5 years post-completion), and the Acceptable Position allows for 5–7 years post-termination/expiry/disclosure. Without a defined duration, the clause falls outside the Acceptable tier and requires escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts.

flags.clause_title: 11 Governing Law. This Agreement shall be governed by and in accordance
flags.snippet: 11 Governing Law. This Agreement shall be governed by and in accordance been independently developed by the Recipient without
flags.rationale: The clause text is incomplete and does not clearly specify the governing law or jurisdiction. This is a critical issue as the governing law must align with UoA Position #POS-16. Preferred governing law is New Zealand, with acceptable alternatives being Australia, the United Kingdom, Common European countries, or Singapore. For US-based clients, non-exclusive jurisdiction of a specific US state is acceptable. The clause must be escalated to General Counsel for review and amendment to ensure compliance.

flags.clause_title: any third party without the disclosing Party’s prior written
flags.snippet: any third party without the disclosing Party’s prior written additional one (1) years after the termination of this Agreement, consent. The Recipient shall treat such Confidential Information provided that any trade sec…
flags.rationale: The clause addresses confidentiality but deviates materially from UoA Position #POS-11. The confidentiality duration is only one year post-termination, which is significantly shorter than the UoA's preferred duration of up to 5 years post-completion or the acceptable range of 5–7 years. Additionally, the clause does not explicitly exclude information already public, independently developed, previously known by the receiving party, or legally required to be disclosed. These omissions fall outside the acceptable tier and require escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts.

flags.clause_title: the Purpose and shall not use the Confidential Information for
flags.snippet: the Purpose and shall not use the Confidential Information for Confidential Information, express or implied, of any kind. The disclosing any other purpose whatsoever. Party shall not be liable for any costs or damages w…
flags.rationale: The clause touches on liability limitations and exclusions but does not align with UoA Position #POS-07. It lacks explicit limitation of liability to direct losses caused by negligence or wilful misconduct, and does not exclude consequential or indirect losses, loss of data/profit/revenue/savings/business/goodwill. Additionally, the clause does not specify a monetary cap, which is critical for compliance with UoA's preferred or acceptable tiers. Escalation to Research Grants and Contracts Delegations is required.

flags.clause_title: Confidential Information pursuant to a court order, subpoena, or other
flags.snippet: Confidential Information pursuant to a court order, subpoena, or other accordance with the ICC Rules in force at the commencement of the governmental authority, Recipient shall provide the disclosing Party arbitration. �
flags.rationale: The clause is unclear and appears to conflate confidentiality obligations with arbitration rules, which are unrelated topics. It does not align with UoA Position #POS-11 on Confidentiality, which requires a clear definition of confidential information, exclusions for public/independently developed/previously known/legally required disclosures, and a time limit (up to 5 years post-completion). The clause also fails to address UoA's ability to disclose to agents and Affiliates. This requires escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts for clarification and redrafting.

flags.clause_title: 2 Confidential information. As used in this Agreement, “Confidential
flags.snippet: 2 Confidential information. As used in this Agreement, “Confidential
flags.rationale: The clause addresses the topic of Confidentiality, which is governed by UoA Position #POS-11. However, the clause text provided is incomplete and does not allow for a full assessment of whether it aligns with the Preferred or Acceptable tier. The Preferred position requires the definition of confidential information to exclude information already public, independently developed, previously known by the receiving party, and information legally required to be disclosed. It also requires UoA to be able to disclose to agents and Affiliates, with a duration of up to 5 years post-completion. The Acceptable tier allows for confidentiality obligations limited to 5–7 years post-termination/expiry/disclosure. Without the full clause text, it is unclear whether these requirements are met, necessitating further review by the Contract Manager.

flags.clause_title: 3.2 The Recipient may only use the Confidential Information for
flags.snippet: 3.2 The Recipient may only use the Confidential Information for
flags.rationale: The clause touches on the topic of Confidentiality, which is governed by UoA Position #POS-11. However, the clause is incomplete and does not provide sufficient detail to confirm alignment with the Preferred or Acceptable tiers. Specifically, it does not address whether the definition of Confidential Information excludes public, independently developed, or previously known information, nor does it specify the duration of confidentiality obligations. This ambiguity requires further review to ensure compliance with UoA's Preferred or Acceptable positions.

However, the clause text provided is incomplete and does not specify whether it aligns with the Preferred or Acceptable tier. Specifically, it is unclear if the definition of Confidential Information excludes information already public, independently developed, previously known by the receiving party, or legally required to be disclosed, as required by the Preferred tier. Additionally, the clause does not address the duration of confidentiality obligations. This ambiguity necessitates further review by the Contract Manager to ensure compliance with UoA's Preferred or Acceptable positions.

flags.clause_title: 5 Return of Confidential Information. Upon the disclosing Party’s
flags.snippet: 5 Return of Confidential Information. Upon the disclosing Party’s request, the Recipient shall promptly return or, at the disclosing Party’s
flags.rationale: The clause addresses the return of confidential information, which falls under the UoA-sensitive topic of Confidentiality (UoA Position #POS-11). However, the clause snippet provided does not clarify whether the definition of confidential information aligns with UoA's Preferred or Acceptable positions (e.g., exclusions for public information, independently developed information, or legally required disclosures). Additionally, the clause does not specify the duration of confidentiality obligations, which is a key requirement under UoA's Preferred and Acceptable tiers. This ambiguity necessitates further review by the Contract Manager to ensure compliance with UoA's confidentiality standards.

flags.clause_title: 6 No License. Any and all rights to the Confidential Information, including
flags.snippet: 6 No License. Any and all rights to the Confidential Information, including Information” means either Party’s non-public and/or proprietary but not limited to copyright, trademarks, patents and any other information tha…
flags.rationale: The clause addresses Intellectual Property (IP) rights, which fall under UoA Position #POS-IP. UoA Contracting Positions explicitly state that all IP clauses must be referred to Auckland UniServices (Head of IP) for review, regardless of wording. This clause does not deviate from the requirement to escalate IP matters.

flags.clause_title: or “proprietary”; (b) if disclosed orally, is designated confidential at

flags.rationale: The clause addresses confidentiality but does not explicitly align with UoA's Preferred Position #POS-11, which requires exclusions for information already public, independently developed, previously known, or legally required to be disclosed. Additionally, the clause does not specify a duration for confidentiality obligations, which is critical for compliance with UoA's Preferred or Acceptable tiers. The clause should be reviewed by the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts to ensure it meets UoA's standards.

flags.clause_title: to its employees, agents, consultants, contractors, and those of
flags.snippet: to its employees, agents, consultants, contractors, and those of completeness of the Confidential Information. its group companies (Representatives) on a strictly need-to-know basis. The Recipient shall ensure that
flags.rationale: The clause addresses the disclosure of confidential information to 'Representatives' on a 'need-to-know' basis, which aligns with the general principle of limiting disclosure. However, the clause does not explicitly address key elements of UoA's Preferred Position on Confidentiality (POS-11), such as the exclusion of public, independently developed, or previously known information, or the ability for UoA to disclose to agents and Affiliates. Additionally, the clause does not specify a time limit for confidentiality obligations. While it does not directly contradict UoA's positions, it falls short of full alignment and requires further review to ensure compliance with UoA's standards.

flags.clause_title: CONFIDENTIALITY AGREEMENT
flags.snippet: CONFIDENTIALITY AGREEMENT This Confidentiality Agreement (the Agreement) is entered into as of the date of the last signature (Effective Date) by and between registered in England and Wales under Company No. and the
flags.rationale: The clause touches on the topic of confidentiality but does not provide sufficient detail to confirm alignment with UoA's Preferred or Acceptable positions. Specifically, it does not address the definition of confidential information, the ability for UoA to disclose to agents and Affiliates, or the duration of confidentiality obligations. These are critical elements outlined in UoA Position #POS-11. The clause requires further review to ensure compliance with UoA's confidentiality standards.

flags.clause_title: 9 Amendments; Entire Agreement. Any amendments to this
flags.snippet: 9 Amendments; Entire Agreement. Any amendments to this bound by obligations no less extensive than those set out in this
flags.rationale: The topic of amendments and entire agreement is not explicitly addressed in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions. As there is no UoA Standard Template for this contract type, this clause falls outside the scope of the documented positions.

flags.snippet: 10 Counterparts; No Assignment. This Agreement may be executed in
flags.rationale: The topic of counterparts and assignment is not addressed in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions document, and there is no UoA Standard Template for this contract type. As such, this clause falls outside the scope of the defined positions.

flags.clause_title: 12 Notices. Any notice hereunder will be effective upon receipt, must be
flags.snippet: 12 Notices. Any notice hereunder will be effective upon receipt, must be appropriate remedy. Recipient shall limit the disclosure of Confidential
flags.rationale: The clause primarily addresses notices and does not clearly align with the confidentiality provisions outlined in UoA Position #POS-11. While it mentions 'Confidential,' the clause does not provide sufficient detail to evaluate its compliance with UoA's confidentiality standards (e.g., exclusions for public information, duration limits, or disclosure to agents and Affiliates). Notices are not explicitly covered by any UoA Preferred Contracting Position, and no standard template exists for this contract type. Therefore, this clause falls outside the scope of the Positions document and is flagged as blue.
flags.standard_ref: Not covered by UoA Position or Template

flags.clause_title: without any warranty as to the correctness or completeness of the
flags.snippet: without any warranty as to the correctness or completeness of the
flags.rationale: The clause aligns with UoA Position #POS-10, which requires the University to expressly exclude all other warranties beyond authority to enter into the contract and performance to a professional standard. This clause explicitly excludes warranties regarding correctness or completeness, which falls within the preferred tier.

flags.clause_title: (a) was known to the Recipient prior to the time of disclosure;
flags.snippet: (a) was known to the Recipient prior to the time of disclosure;
flags.rationale: The clause relates to the definition of confidential information, specifically excluding information already known to the recipient prior to disclosure. This aligns with UoA Preferred Contracting Position POS-11, which specifies that the definition of confidential information should exclude such information. The clause matches the Preferred tier.

flags.clause_title: with the laws of England. Any dispute arising out of or in connection
flags.snippet: with the laws of England. Any dispute arising out of or in connection reference to or use of the Confidential Information. with this Agreement, including any question regarding its existence,
flags.rationale: The clause specifies that the governing law is England, which is explicitly listed as acceptable under UoA Position #POS-16. The clause does not require escalation as it aligns with the Acceptable tier for Governing Law and Jurisdiction.
